claim: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standardized protocol enabling clients (IDE, Claude, ChatGPT) to access tools and data from servers without reimplementing integrations
actionable: Build MCP servers instead of direct API integrations; enables tool reuse across multiple AI clients
source_title: What is MCP? (simplest explanation + how to use it)

claim: MCP servers handle complex communication with different tools/APIs and manage personal tokens/credentials securely, abstracting complexity from clients
actionable: Create MCP servers to centralize tool integrations and credential management; multiple clients can reuse the same server
source_title: What is MCP? (simplest explanation + how to use it)

claim: Zapier's MCP service allows no-code setup of MCP servers; connects to thousands of apps, handling integrations without custom code
actionable: Use Zapier MCP for quick no-code agent integrations; customize your own personal MCP server in minutes
source_title: What is MCP? (simplest explanation + how to use it)

claim: Each AI client connects to MCP servers differently; Cursor uses command-shift-J, others differ; consult client setup docs for MCP configuration
actionable: Check client-specific setup instructions for MCP; integration method varies (Cursor, VSCode, ChatGPT); JSON config typically required
source_title: What is MCP? (simplest explanation + how to use it)
